workflow_fidelity_facets.text: Probe discovery workflow explains dye libraries, screening, hit selection, and confocal or microscopy-based bacteria-dye testing.
Performance evidence covers spectral properties, fluorescence response, response time, limit of detection, sensitivity, or specificity.
Mechanism evidence covers lipopolysaccharide, LPS, outer membrane, lipoprotein, or membrane interaction uncertainty.
Validation evidence covers infected plant samples, field or sample validation, controls, selectivity, specificity, or off-target bacteria.
AI acceleration evidence covers public databases, molecular or spectral prediction, computational experiments, local data architecture, and validation loop.

final_report_usefulness_facets.text: Final report distinguishes what the anchor papers did, what is inferred from evidence, and what is proposed as AI acceleration.
Final report inventories public chemical, spectral, bacterial, literature, or assay databases with access method and useful fields.
Final report provides practical local data architecture with tables, columns, compounds, spectra, bacterial targets, assay outcomes, or model features.
Final report discloses gaps, uncertainty, failure modes, false positives, controls, or selectivity risks.

expected_claims.text: B-1 is a turn-on fluorescent probe for detection of Erwinia amylovora, the fire blight pathogen, anchored to iScience DOI 10.1016/j.isci.2023.106557.
The companion STAR Protocols paper describes a B-1 probe detection protocol for Erwinia amylovora, anchored to DOI 10.1016/j.xpro.2023.102412.

Field or sample-validation claims should cite performance evidence for B-1 detection, such as sensitivity, specificity, limit of detection, fluorescence response, or validation against infected plant samples.

source_backed_evidence.title: A turn-on fluorescent probe for the detection of Erwinia amylovora and fire blight diagnosis
source_backed_evidence.quote: B-1 is a turn-on fluorescent probe for detection of Erwinia amylovora, the fire blight pathogen; the iScience paper DOI 10.1016/j.isci.2023.106557 reports fluorescence response and validation with infected plant samples.

source_backed_evidence.query: B-1 Erwinia amylovora fire blight fluorescent probe 10.1016/j.isci.2023.106557
source_backed_evidence.title: B-1 fluorescent probe discovery workflow using dye-library screening
source_backed_evidence.quote: The iScience workflow for B-1 probe discovery used dye-library screening, bacteria-dye combination screening, confocal microscopy, and hit selection for Erwinia amylovora fluorescence response.

source_backed_evidence.query: B-1 Erwinia amylovora fire blight fluorescent probe 10.1016/j.isci.2023.106557
source_backed_evidence.title: B-1 spectra, detection limit, response time, and selectivity evidence
source_backed_evidence.quote: B-1 performance evidence includes spectral excitation and emission behavior, fluorescence response, limit of detection near 10^2 CFU/mL, rapid response time, sensitivity, specificity, and selectivity against off-target bacteria.

source_backed_evidence.query: B-1 Erwinia amylovora fire blight fluorescent probe 10.1016/j.isci.2023.106557
source_backed_evidence.title: B-1 interaction with LPS, lipopolysaccharide, outer membrane, and lipoprotein targets
source_backed_evidence.quote: Mechanistic evidence links B-1 fluorescence turn-on to Erwinia amylovora lipopolysaccharide (LPS), outer membrane environment, and a lipoprotein interaction, while the exact binding site remains uncertain.
